Tr+1=Crnan-rbr

Therefore, 13-3r=7⇒r=2 for coefficient of x7.

⇒Tr+1=Cr13a13-rx13-3rb-r

Therefore, 13-3r=-5 for coefficient of x-5

⇒r=6

So,

C213a11b2=C613a7b-6

q: The coefficient of $x^2$ in the expansion of $\left(2x^2 + \dfrac{1}{x}\right)^{10}$, $x \neq 0$, is :
question: The coefficient of $x^2$ in the expansion of $\left(2x^2 + \dfrac{1}{x}\right)^{10}$, $x \neq 0$, is :
solution:
The general term in the expansion of $\left(2x^2 + \dfrac{1}{x}\right)^{10}$ is given by:

$T_{r+1} = ^{10}C_{r} (2x^2)^{10-r} \left(\dfrac{1}{x}\right)^r$

$T_{r+1} = ^{10}C_{r} 2^{10-r} x^{20-2r} x^{-r}$

$T_{r+1} = ^{10}C_{r} 2^{10-r} x^{20-3r}$

For the coefficient of $x^2$, we equate the power of $x$ to $2$:

$20 - 3r = 2$

$3r = 18 \Rightarrow r = 6$

Substituting $r = 6$ in the coefficient part, we get:

Coefficient $= ^{10}C_{6} 2^{10-6}$

$= ^{10}C_{4} 2^4$

$= \dfrac{10 \times 9 \times 8 \times 7}{4 \times 3 \times 2 \times 1} \times 16$

$= 210 \times 16$

$= 3360$

Answer: $3360$

q: If for $3 \leq r \leq 30$, $\binom{30}{30-r} + 3\binom{30}{31-r} + 3\binom{30}{32-r} + \binom{30}{33-r} = \binom{m}{r}$, then $m$ equals:
question: If for $3 \leq r \leq 30$, $\binom{30}{30-r} + 3\binom{30}{31-r} + 3\binom{30}{32-r} + \binom{30}{33-r} = \binom{m}{r}$, then $m$ equals:
solution:
Using the property $^{n}C_{r} = ^{n}C_{n-r}$, the given expression can be written as:

$^{30}C_{r} + 3 \cdot ^{30}C_{r-1} + 3 \cdot ^{30}C_{r-2} + ^{30}C_{r-3}$

This expression represents the coefficient of $x^r$ in the expansion of:

$(1+x)^{30} + 3x(1+x)^{30} + 3x^2(1+x)^{30} + x^3(1+x)^{30}$

Taking $(1+x)^{30}$ common, we get:

$(1+x)^{30}(1 + 3x + 3x^2 + x^3)$

$= (1+x)^{30}(1+x)^3$

$= (1+x)^{33}$

The coefficient of $x^r$ in $(1+x)^{33}$ is $^{33}C_{r}$.

Comparing this with $^{m}C_{r}$, we get $m = 33$.

Answer: $33$

solution:
We have been given that C32n:C3n=10

We know that Crn=n!r!n-r!

⇒2n!3!2n-3!n!3!n-3!=10

⇒2n!3!2n-3!×3!n-3!n!=10

⇒2n2n-12n-2nn-1n-2=10

⇒42n-1n-2=10

⇒8n-4=10n-20

⇒n=8

Hence, the value of n2+3nn2-3n+4 is =82+3882-38+4=8844=2

Therefore, the value of n2+3nn2-3n+4 is 2:1.

q: If $\sum_{r=1}^{30} \frac{r^2\left({ }^{30} C_r\right)^2}{{ }^{30} C_{r-1}}=\alpha \times 2^{29}$, then $\alpha$ is equal to ______
question: If $\sum_{r=1}^{30} \frac{r^2\left({ }^{30} C_r\right)^2}{{ }^{30} C_{r-1}}=\alpha \times 2^{29}$, then $\alpha$ is equal to ______
solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \sum_{\mathrm{r}=1}^{30} \frac{\mathrm{r}^2\left({ }^{30} \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}}\right)^2}{{ }^{30} \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}-1}} \\ & =\sum_{\mathrm{r}=1}^{30} \mathrm{r}^2\left(\frac{31-\mathrm{r}}{\mathrm{r}}\right) \cdot \frac{30!}{\mathrm{r}!(30-\mathrm{r})!} \\ & \left(\because \frac{{ }^{30} \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}}}{{ }^{30} \mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{r}-1}}=\frac{30-\mathrm{r}+1}{\mathrm{r}}=\frac{31-\mathrm{r}}{\mathrm{r}}\right)\end{aligned}$


$\begin{aligned} & =\sum_{\mathrm{r}=1}^{30} \frac{(31-\mathrm{r}) 30!}{(\mathrm{r}-1)!(30-\mathrm{r})!} \\ & =30 \sum_{\mathrm{r}=1}^{30} \frac{(31-\mathrm{r}) 29!}{(\mathrm{r}-1)!(30-\mathrm{r})!} \\ & =30 \sum_{\mathrm{r}=1}^{30}(30-\mathrm{r}+1)^{29} \mathrm{C}_{30-\mathrm{r}} \\ & =30\left(\sum_{\mathrm{r}=1}^{30}(31-\mathrm{r})^{29} \mathrm{C}_{30-\mathrm{r}}+\sum_{\mathrm{r}=1}^{30}{ }^{29} \mathrm{C}_{30-\mathrm{r}}\right) \\ & =30\left(29 \times 2^{28}+2^{29}\right)=30(29+2) 2^{28} \\ & =15 \times 31 \times 2^{29} \\ & =465\left(2^{29}\right) \\ & \alpha=465\end{aligned}$

q: The coefficient of $x^{10}$ in the expansion of $(1+x)^2$ $\left(1+x^2\right)^3\left(1+x^3\right)^4$ is equal to
question: The coefficient of $x^{10}$ in the expansion of $(1+x)^2$ $\left(1+x^2\right)^3\left(1+x^3\right)^4$ is equal to
solution:
$\because(1+x)^2=1+2 x+x^2$,
$$
\left(1+x^2\right)^3=1+3 x^2+3 x^4+x^6,
$$
and $\left(1+x^3\right)^4=1+4 x^3+6 x^6+4 x^9+x^{12}$
So, the possible combinations for $x^{10}$ are:
$$
x \cdot x^9, x \cdot x^6 \cdot x^3, x^2 \cdot x^2 \cdot x^6, x^4 \cdot x^6
$$
Corresponding coefficients are $2 \times 4,2 \times 1 \times$ $4,1 \times 3 \times 6,3 \times 6$ or $8,8,18,18$.
$\therefore$ Sum of the coefficient is
$$
8+8+18+18=52
$$
Therefore, the coefficient of $x^{10}$ in the expansion of $(1+x)^2\left(1+x^2\right)^3\left(1+x^3\right)^4$ is 52 .

question: In the binomial expansion of $(a-b)^n, n \geq 5$, the sum of $5^{\text {th }}$ and $6^{\text {th }}$ terms is zero, then $\frac{a}{b}$ equals
solution:
${ }^n C_4 a^{n-4}(-b)^4+{ }^n C_5 a^{n-5}(-b)^5=0$
$\Rightarrow\left(\frac{a}{b}\right)=\frac{n-5+1}{5}$.

q: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mn>25</mn><mn>190</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><msup><mn>19</mn><mn>190</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><msup><mn>8</mn><mn>190</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mn>2</mn><mn>190</mn></msup></math> is divisible by
question: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mn>25</mn><mn>190</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><msup><mn>19</mn><mn>190</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><msup><mn>8</mn><mn>190</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mn>2</mn><mn>190</mn></msup></math> is divisible by
solution:
Given,

25190-19190-8190+2190

Now  25190-19190, 8190, 2190 are divisible by 2, as 25190 will give remainder as 1 and 19190 will give remainder as 1 when divided by 2, so combined 25190-19190 will give remainder 0 

Now, 25190-8190 & -19190+2190 are divisible by 17, an-bn is divisible by a-b if n is even, 
So given number is divisible by 34 as it is divisible by 2 & 17 both,

Now,25190=7×3+4190, dividing by 7 we get,

7×3+4190=4190=1695=295=4·831

Now when 4·7+131 divided by 7 will give remainder as 4·1=4

And 19190=7×3-2190, when divided by 7 we get,

7×3-2190=-2190=2190=2×863=2×7+163

So, when 2×7+163 divided by 7 will give remainder as 2

Similarly 8190=7+1190 will give 1 remainder 

And 2190=27+163 will give 1 remainder,

So combining all we get,25190-19190-8190+2190=4-2-1+1=5
⇒Not divisible by 7, so it will not be divisible by 14

solution:
Let coefficient of first term is Crn 

CrnCr+1n=17

⇒ r+1n-r=17

⇒ 7r+7=n-r

n-8r=7       …1 

Also,  Cr+1nCr+2n=742=16

⇒r+2n-r-1=16

⇒6r+12=n-r-1

n-7r=13       …2 

Solving 1 and 2, we get r=6

Hence, 7th term is the answer.
